steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank introduces a sentence about snowpack at higher elevations after the passage has just discussed snowpack at lower elevations. The two elevation zones show different conditions, so the transition must signal a contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — [signals explanation or elaboration]: This implies the higher-elevation finding explains the lower-elevation finding — it does not. ✗
  • Choice B — [signals example]: The higher-elevation data is not an example of the lower-elevation trend; it contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'on the other hand': This directly signals contrast between the two elevation zones, matching the shift in both timing (early March vs. mid-March) and conditions. ✓
  • Choice D — [signals conclusion or restatement]: The second sentence introduces new information rather than summarizing what came before. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
